title: Modified NEBNext VarSkip Short SARS-CoV-2 Library Prep for Wastewater Samples
author: Padmini Ramachandran
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.b34qqqvw

steps.title: Gently mix and spin down the LunaScript RT SuperMix reagent
steps.instruction: Gently mix and spin down the LunaScript RT SuperMix reagent. Prepare the cDNA synthesis reaction as described below: %s %s For no template controls, mix the following components: %s %s

steps.title: Incubate reactions in a thermocycler* with...
steps.instruction: Incubate reactions in a thermocycler* with the following steps: A B C D 1 CYCLE STEP TEMP TIME CYCLES 2 Primer Annealing 25°C 2 minutes 1 3 cDNA Synthesis 55°C 20 minutes 4 Heat Inactivation 95°C 1 minute 5 Hold 4°C ∞ *Set heated lid to 105°C Samples can be stored at -20 °C for up to a week.

4.5 µl cDNA input is recommended. If using less than 4.5 µl of cDNA, add nuclease-free water to a final volume of 4.5 µl. We recommend setting up the cDNA synthesis and cDNA amplification reactions in different rooms to minimize cross-contamination of future reactions. The cDNA reaction can be set up in hood to minimize cross-contamination.

steps.title: Gently mix and spin down reagents
steps.instruction: Gently mix and spin down reagents. Prepare the split pool cDNA amplification reactions as described below: For Pool Set A: A B 1 COMPONENT VOLUME 2 cDNA (Step 2) 4.5 µl 3 (lilac) Q5 Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix 6.25 µl 4 NEBNext VarSkip Short SARS-CoV-2 Primer Mix 1 1.75 µl 5 Total Volume 12.5 µl For Pool Set B: A B 1 COMPONENT VOLUME 2 cDNA (Step 2) 4.5 µl 3 (lilac) Q5 Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix 6.25 µl 4 NEBNext VarSkip Short SARS-CoV-2 Primer Mix 2 1.75 µl 5 Total Volume 12.5 µl

steps.title: Incubate reactions in a thermocycler* with...
steps.instruction: Incubate reactions in a thermocycler* with the following steps: A B C D 1 CYCLE STEP TEMP TIME CYCLES 2 Initial Denaturation 98°C 30 seconds 1 3 Denature 95°C 15 seconds 35 4 Annealing/Extension 63°C 5 minutes 5 Hold 4°C ∞ 1 *Set heated lid to 105°C

steps.title: Combine the Pool A and Pool...
steps.instruction: Combine the Pool A and Pool B PCR reactions for each sample. Samples can be stored at -20 °C for up to a week.

steps.title: The volume of NEBNext Sample Purification...
steps.instruction: The volume of NEBNext Sample Purification Beads provided here are for use with the sample composition at this step (25 µl; Step 6). These bead volumes may not work properly for a cleanup at a different step in the workflow, or if this is a second cleanup at this step. For cleanups of samples contained in different buffer conditions the volumes may need to be experimentally determined.

steps.title: Vortex the NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to resuspend
steps.instruction: Vortex the NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to resuspend.

steps.title: Add 20 µL, (0
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL, (0.8X) resuspended beads to the combined PCR reaction. Mix well by pipetting up and down at least 10 times. Be careful to expel all of the liquid out of the tip during the last mix. Vortexing for 3-5 seconds on high can also be used. If centrifuging samples after mixing, be sure to stop the centrifugation before the beads start to settle out.

steps.title: Incubate samples at Room temperature for at least 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate samples at Room temperature for at least 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Place the tube/plate on an appropriate...
steps.instruction: Place the tube/plate on an appropriate magnetic stand to separate the beads from the supernatant.

steps.title: After 5 minutes (or when the...
steps.instruction: After 5 minutes (or when the solution is clear), carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets. Caution: do not discard the beads .

steps.title: Add 200 µL, 80% freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL, 80% freshly prepared ethanol to the tube/plate while in the magnetic stand. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:00:30 , and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets.

steps.title: Perform second Ethanol wash
steps.instruction: Perform second Ethanol wash: Add 200 µL, 80% freshly prepared ethanol to the tube/plate while in the magnetic stand. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:00:30 , and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets. Be sure to remove all visible liquid after the second wash. If necessary, briefly spin the tube/plate, place back on the magnet and remove traces of ethanol with a p10 pipette tip.

steps.title: Air dry the beads for up...
steps.instruction: Air dry the beads for up to 5 minutes while the tube/plate is on the magnetic stand with the lid open. Caution: Do not over-dry the beads. This may result in lower recovery of DNA. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack, they are too dry.

steps.title: Remove the tube/plate from the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Remove the tube/plate from the magnetic stand. Elute the DNA target from the beads by adding 28 µL, 0.1X TE . If not assessing cDNA (Step 24) elute DNA in 27 µL, 0.1X TE .

steps.title: Mix well by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix well by pipetting up and down 10 times, or on a vortex mixer. Incubate for at least 2 minutes at Room temperature . If necessary, quickly spin the sample to collect the liquid from the sides of the tube or plate wells before placing back on the magnetic stand.

steps.title: Place the tube/plate on the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Place the tube/plate on the magnetic stand. After 5 minutes (or when the solution is clear), transfer 26 µL to a new PCR tube. If not assessing cDNA (Step 24) transfer 25 µL to a new PCR tube.

steps.title: We recommend assessing cDNA amplicon (from...
steps.instruction: We recommend assessing cDNA amplicon (from Step 23) concentrations with a Qubit fluorometer using Qubit HS DNA assay kit. Amplicons may also be run on a Bioanalyzer or TapeStation ® to confirm ~500 bp size of amplicons. To run on a Bioanalyzer, dilute amplicon 10-fold with 0.1X TE Buffer and run 2 µL on a DNA High Sensitivity ScreenTape. (See Figure 19 below for example of amplicon size profile on a Tapestation). Figure 19: Example of cDNA amplicons generated from 1000 genome copies of SARS CoV-2 in the absence (A) and presence (B) of human primer controls. Samples can be stored at -20 °C for up to a week.

steps.title: Add the following components to a sterile nuclease-free tube
steps.instruction: Add the following components to a sterile nuclease-free tube: A B 1 COMPONENT VOLUME 2 (green) NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Enzyme Mix 1.5 µl 3 (green) NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Reaction Buffer 3.5 µl 4 Targeted cDNA Amplicons* 25 µl 5 Total Volume 30 µl *When cleanup of the pooled cDNA amplicons is skipped, the amplicons must be diluted prior to library prep, please dilute the cDNA as mentioned here. Transfer 2.5 µl of the pooled cDNA amplicons to a fresh tube. Add 22.5 µl of 0.1X TE for a final volume of 25 µl. If cleanup is performed, use the cleaned targeted cDNA amplicons as the input.

steps.title: Set a 100 µl or 200...
steps.instruction: Set a 100 µl or 200 µl pipette to 25 µl and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube. It is important to mix well. The presence of a small amount of bubbles will not interfere with performance

steps.title: Place in a thermocycler* and run the following program
steps.instruction: Place in a thermocycler* and run the following program: %s %s If necessary, samples can be stored at -20 °C ; however, a slight loss in yield (~20%) may be observed. We recommend continuing with adaptor ligation before stopping.

steps.title: Add the following components directly to...
steps.instruction: Add the following components directly to the End Prep Reaction Mixture: %s %s Do not premix adaptor with the Ligation Master Mix.

steps.title: Set a 100 µl or 200...
steps.instruction: Set a 100 µl or 200 µl pipette to 40 µl and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube. Caution: The NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix is very viscous. Care should be taken to ensure adequate mixing of the ligation reaction, as incomplete mixing will result in reduced ligation efficiency. The presence of a small amount of bubbles will not interfere with performance.

steps.title: Incubate at 20 °C for 00:15:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 20 °C for 00:15:00 in a thermocycler with the heated lid off.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5 µL, (red or blue) USER® Enzyme to the ligation mixture from the previous step. Steps 26 and 27 are only required for use with NEBNext non-indexed Adaptors. USER enzyme can be found in the NEBNext Multiplex Oligos ( www.neb.com/oligos ).

steps.title: Mix well and incubate at 37...
steps.instruction: Mix well and incubate at 37 °C for 00:15:00 with the heated lid set to ≥ 47 °C . Samples can be stored overnight at -20 °C .

steps.title: The following section is for cleanup of the ligation reaction
steps.instruction: The following section is for cleanup of the ligation reaction. The volume of NEBNext Sample Purification Beads provided here are for use with the sample contained in the exact buffer at this step (47.75 µl; Step 27). These bead volumes may not work properly for a cleanup at a different step in the workflow, or if this is a second cleanup at this step. For cleanups of samples contained in different buffer conditions the volumes may need to be experimentally determined.

steps.title: Vortex the NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to resuspend
steps.instruction: Vortex the NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to resuspend.

steps.title: Add 43 µL, (0
steps.instruction: Add 43 µL, (0.9X) resuspended beads to the Adaptor Ligation reaction. Mix well by pipetting up and down at least 10 times. Be careful to expel all of the liquid out of the tip during the last mix. Vortexing for 3-5 seconds on high can also be used. If centrifuging samples after mixing, be sure to stop the centrifugation before the beads start to settle out.

steps.title: Incubate samples on bench top for...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples on bench top for at least 00:05:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Place the tube/plate on an appropriate...
steps.instruction: Place the tube/plate on an appropriate magnetic stand to separate the beads from the supernatant.

steps.title: After 5 minutes (or when the...
steps.instruction: After 5 minutes (or when the solution is clear), carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets. Caution: do not discard beads .

steps.title: Add 200 µL, 80% freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL, 80% freshly prepared ethanol to the tube/ plate while in the magnetic stand. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:00:30 , and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets.

steps.title: Repeat the previous step once for...
steps.instruction: Repeat the previous step once for a total of two washes: Add 200 µL, 80% freshly prepared ethanol to the tube/ plate while in the magnetic stand. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:00:30 , and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets. Be sure to remove all visible liquid after the second wash. If necessary, briefly spin the tube/plate, place back on the magnet and remove traces of ethanol with a p10 pipette tip.

steps.title: Air dry the beads for up...
steps.instruction: Air dry the beads for up to 5 minutes while the tube/plate is on the magnetic stand with the lid open. Caution: Do not over-dry the beads. This may result in lower recovery of DNA target. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack, they are too dry.

steps.title: Remove the tube/plate from the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Remove the tube/plate from the magnetic stand. Elute the DNA target from the beads by adding 10 µL, 0.1X TE .

steps.title: Mix well by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix well by pipetting up and down 10 times, or on a vortex mixer. Incubate for at least 00:02:00 at Room temperature . If necessary, quickly spin the sample to collect the liquid from the sides of the tube or plate wells before placing back on the magnetic stand.

steps.title: Place the tube/plate on the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Place the tube/plate on the magnetic stand. After 00:05:00 (or when the solution is clear), transfer 7.5 µL to a new PCR tube. Samples can be stored at -20 °C .

steps.title: We only recommend using NEBNext Oligo...
steps.instruction: We only recommend using NEBNext Oligo kit where index primers are supplied in a 96-well plate format. These kits have the forward and reverse (i7 and i5) primers combined. Primers are supplied ay 10 µM (combined). We observed addtion of spurious nucleotides when using primers supplied in separate tubes

steps.title: Add the following components to a sterile strip tube
steps.instruction: Add the following components to a sterile strip tube: A B 1 COMPONENT VOLUME 2 Adaptor Ligated DNA Fragments (Step 45) 7.5 μl 3 (blue) NEBNext Library PCR Master Mix 12.5 μl 4 Index Primer Mix (E6440S)* 5.0 μl 5 Total Volume 25 μl * NEBNext Oligos must be purchased separately from the library prep kit. Refer to the corresponding NEBNext Oligo kit manual for determining valid barcode combinations.

steps.title: Set a 100 μl pipette to...
steps.instruction: Set a 100 μl pipette to 20 μl and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube.

steps.title: Place the tube on a thermocycler**...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a thermocycler** and perform PCR amplification using the following PCR cycling conditions: %s %s

steps.title: The NEBNext Sample Purification Beads provided...
steps.instruction: The NEBNext Sample Purification Beads provided here are for use with the sample contained in the exact buffer at this step. These volumes may not work properly for a cleanup at a different step in the workflow. For cleanups of samples contained in different buffer conditions the volumes may need to be experimentally determined.

steps.title: Vortex NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to resuspend
steps.instruction: Vortex NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to resuspend.

steps.title: Add 22
steps.instruction: Add 22.5 µL, (0.9X) resuspended beads to the PCR reaction. Mix well by pipetting up and down at least 10 times. Be careful to expel all of the liquid out of the tip during the last mix. Vortexing for 3-5 seconds on high can also be used. If centrifuging samples after mixing, be sure to stop the centrifugation before the beads start to settle out.

steps.title: Incubate samples on bench top for...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples on bench top for at least 00:05:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Place the tube/plate on an appropriate...
steps.instruction: Place the tube/plate on an appropriate magnetic stand to separate the beads from the supernatant.

steps.title: After 5 minutes (or when the...
steps.instruction: After 5 minutes (or when the solution is clear), carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets. Caution: do not discard the beads.

steps.title: Add 200 µL, 80% freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL, 80% freshly prepared ethanol to the tube/plate while in the magnetic stand. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:00:30 , and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets.

steps.title: Repeat the previous step once for...
steps.instruction: Repeat the previous step once for a total of two washes: Add 200 µL, 80% freshly prepared ethanol to the tube/plate while in the magnetic stand. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:00:30 , and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets. Be sure to remove all visible liquid after the second wash. If necessary, briefly spin the tube/plate, place back on the magnet and remove traces of ethanol with a p10 pipette tip.

steps.title: Air dry the beads for up...
steps.instruction: Air dry the beads for up to 5 minutes while the tube/plate is on the magnetic stand with the lid open. Caution: Do not over-dry the beads. This may result in lower recovery of DNA. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack, they are too dry.

steps.title: Remove the tube/plate from the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Remove the tube/plate from the magnetic stand. Elute the DNA target from the beads by adding 17 µL, 0.1X TE .

steps.title: Mix well by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix well by pipetting up and down 10 times, or on a vortex mixer. Incubate for at least 00:02:00 at Room temperature . If necessary, quickly spin the sample to collect the liquid from the sides of the tube or plate wells before placing back on the magnetic stand.

steps.title: Place the tube/plate on the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Place the tube/plate on the magnetic stand. After 5 minutes (or when the solution is clear), transfer 15 µL to a new PCR tube and store at -20 °C .

steps.title: Check the size distribution on an Agilent Bioanalyzer or TapeStation
steps.instruction: Check the size distribution on an Agilent Bioanalyzer or TapeStation. The sample may need to be diluted before loading. A peak size of ~520 bp is expected (Figure 56). Samples can be stored at -20 °C or proceed to MiSeq denature and dilution guideline to load it on to the MiSeq. Figure 56: Example of final library size distributions on a TapeStation. ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 libraries were generated from 1000 viral copies in the absence (A) or presence (B) of the human primer controls.

steps.title: Flick the tubes or pipet up...
steps.instruction: Flick the tubes or pipet up and down 10 times to mix followed by a quick spin.

steps.title: Flick the tubes or pipet up...
steps.instruction: Flick the tubes or pipet up and down 10 times to mix followed by a quick spin.

steps.title: Assess the quantity of the cDNA...
steps.instruction: Assess the quantity of the cDNA amplicon using the Qubit HS kit. ( 1 µL, each combined pool

steps.title: Based on the viral load in...
steps.instruction: Based on the viral load in the sample, you will see a spectrum of fragment profiles. If there is a single peak around 400-600 bp, the clean-up step can be omitted and you can proceed directly to library preparation using Illumina DNA Prep https://www.protocols.io/edit/modified-illumina-dna-prep-m-tagmentation-library-b2yzqfx6 If there is not a single peak, check the amplicon size distributions. If there are lots of smaller peaks, for example, we typically see one at 44, 75, 128, and 213, these PCR reaction pools need to be cleaned up. Follow the cleanup protocol and prepare libraries using the Ultra II DNA workflow by NEB Next ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Library Prep Kit (Illumina). If you are not able to perform the PCR pool assessment above, proceed to PCR cleanup and prepare libraries using the Ultra II DNA workflow by NEB Next ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Library Prep Kit (Illumina).

steps.title: This is library preparation Option 1,...
steps.instruction: This is library preparation Option 1, and the most common that will be used. You will use the Ultra II DNA workflow as described in the NEBNext® ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Library Prep Kit (Illumina®) (cat# E7650S/L), with NEBNext ® Multiplex Oligos for Illumina ® (96 Unique Dual Index Primer Pairs) (cat# E6440S/L).

steps.title: Sample Sheet generation for NEBNext ®...
steps.instruction: Sample Sheet generation for NEBNext ® ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Library Prep Kit (Illumina ® ) with E6440 Index plate. E6440_ Forward Strand Workflow Sample Sheet.csv

steps.title: The presence of genomic DNA or...
steps.instruction: The presence of genomic DNA or carry-over products can interfere with sequencing accuracy, particularly for low copy targets. Therefore, it is important to carry out the appropriate no template control (NTC) reactions to demonstrate that positive reactions are meaningful. We have also verified cDNA synthesis using the Invitrogen™ SuperScript™ IV First-Strand Synthesis System (Catalog number:18091200), as described in the SNAP protocol with modifications (random hexamers, RT incubation of 30 min.).

steps.title: We have observed the following output...
steps.instruction: We have observed the following output for VSS amplicons based on viral titer in the wastewater samples: 1)Moderate to high viral titer samples: The cleaned amplicon quantity is expected to be 0.5 ng/µl to 8 ng/µl 2)Low viral titer samples: The cleaned amplicon quantity is expected to be 0 - 0.2 ng/µl We recommend to proceed to library preparation for samples at 0.2 ng/µl or higher. Cleaned VSS amplicons below this value have not yielded sequencing results.

steps.instruction: on Tape Station or Bioanalyzer using High Sensitivity kit. Figure

steps.instruction: Tape station profile of cleaned amplicon from a moderate to high positive sample. Figure

steps.title: Tape station profile of cleaned amplicon...
steps.instruction: Tape station profile of cleaned amplicon from a low positive sample measured at ~0 ng/ul.
